what: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: goal datey … standardise annual, .e. year-based calculations e.g. required actuarial mortality experience analyses valuations. Fixed precision dates calendar years [1000, 3000) Technically Proleptic Gregorian calendar Guarantees: Can represent start middle day leap year non leap year Years divisible 120 can convert standard base R dates (Date, POSIXct POSIXlt). Key argument day_fraction. Simpler just use start_day, mid_day end_day. instance start E2R typically included, use start_day. individual survived end E2R also typically included, use end_day. means E2R comprises separate periods interlock correctly (E2Rs measurable) individual died average final day use mid_day.

title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: can install development version datey GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("logmu-org/r-datey")

title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: basic example shows solve common problem: special using README.Rmd instead just README.md? can include R chunks like : ’ll still need render README.Rmd regularly, keep README.md --date. devtools::build_readme() handy . can also embed plots man/figures folder, example:  case, don’t forget commit push resulting figure files, display GitHub CRAN.
code: library(datey) ## basic example code summary(cars) #>      speed           dist        #>  Min.   : 4.0   Min.   :  2.00   #>  1st Qu.:12.0   1st Qu.: 26.00   #>  Median :15.0   Median : 36.00   #>  Mean   :15.4   Mean   : 42.98   #>  3rd Qu.:19.0   3rd Qu.: 56.00   #>  Max.   :25.0   Max.   :120.00

what: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf
title: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf
text: as_ymdf() returns list year, month, day day_fraction breakdown datey, year integer [1000,3000), month integer [1,12], day integer [1,N], N number days month specified year month, day_fraction double [0,1) representing fraction day, e.g. 0 means start 0.5 means middle day. datey constructed using end_day day_fraction = 1 as_ymdf() return start next day day_fraction = 0.

title: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf
code: as_ymdf(datey)

title: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf
text: datey datey deconstruct.

title: Handling invalid dates — datey.NA
text: Valid dates within datey system calendar years interval [1000,3000). Dates outside interval treated NA. Use .na() test whether datey NA element, anyNA() test whether element datey NA. datey version NA NA_datey_. convenience, following constants also available: valid_years_start: first valid calendar year (1000). valid_years_end: first invalid calendar year valid_years_start (3000). performance reasons, intermediate calculations may check NAs.

title: Handling invalid dates — datey.NA
code: NA_datey_  # S3 method for class 'datey' is.na(x)  # S3 method for class 'datey' anyNA(x, recursive = FALSE)  valid_years_start  valid_years_end

title: Handling invalid dates — datey.NA
text: object class datey_type (inherits datey) length 1. object class integer length 1. object class integer length 1.

text: x datey test validity. recursive Unused.

title: Handling invalid dates — datey.NA
text: single logical value, TRUE FALSE, never NA never anything single value.

title: Handling invalid dates — datey.NA
code: x <- c(NA_datey_, datey(2000))   is.na(x) # c(TRUE, FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE   anyNA(x) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Parse text as a datey — datey.character
title: Parse text as a datey — datey.character
text: day_fraction provided text must ISO 8601 extended format, .e. "YYYY-MM-DD". day_fraction provided text must formatted "YYYY-MM-DD.FFF", ".FFF" optional day fraction. means e.g. "2000-01-01" represents start 1 January 2000. blank_is_NA TRUE blanks treated NA. strict TRUE (default) non-compliant text stop execution. lengths vector arguments must multiples .

title: Parse text as a datey — datey.character
code: # S3 method for class 'character' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, blank_is_NA = FALSE, ...)

title: Parse text as a datey — datey.character
text: x Vector text items parsed. day_fraction day_fraction override. Defaults NULL. day_fraction provided x used derive calendar year, month, day day fraction. day_fraction provided x used solely derive calendar year, month day, day_fraction provides position day. day_fraction must lie inclusive interval [0,1], 0 meaning start day, 0.5 meaning middle day, 1 meaning end day (identical start next day). strict non-compliant text (including calendar years less 1000 greater equal 3000) handled. strict TRUE execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. Defaults TRUE. blank_is_NA Whether blanks treated NA. Defaults FALSE. ... arguments (used package).

title: Creates a datey — datey
text: package provides methods create datey following: double integer interpreted specified calendar year, fractional part representing fraction year. instance, datey(2000.5) means halfway though year 2000. (means integer argument always indicates start calendar year, e.g. datey(2000L) start year 2000.) Date POSIXct POSIXlt interpreted fractional years. day_fraction argument provided day fraction determined hours, minutes, seconds. instance, datey(.POSIXct("2000-03-21 12:00")) means middle 2000-03-21. Note standard use, Date fractional part therefore means start day. instance, datey(.Date("2000-03-21 12:00")) means start 2000-03-21. character – day_fraction provided text format must ISO 8601 extended format, .e. YYYY-MM-DD. day_fraction provided text format must YYYY-MM-DD.FFF, .FFF day fraction must present even fraction 0, e.g. "2000-01-01.0" start 1 January 2000. datey interpreted optional day_fraction override. Note day_fraction 1 add day day boundary, even originally defined end day. lengths vector arguments must multiples . S3 generic.

title: Creates a datey — datey
code: datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # Default S3 method datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'integer' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'double' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Date' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXct' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXlt' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  as_start_day(x, ...)  as_mid_day(x, ...)  as_end_day(x, ...)

title: Creates a datey — datey
text: x argument convert datey. day_fraction day_fraction override. Defaults NULL. day_fraction provided x used derive calendar year, month, day day fraction. day_fraction provided x used solely derive calendar year, month day, day_fraction provides position day. day_fraction must lie inclusive interval [0,1], 0 meaning start day, 0.5 meaning middle day, 1 meaning end day (identical start next day). strict calendar years less 1000 greater equal 3000 day fractions interval [0,1] handled. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NAs result NA regardless switch.) ... arguments (used package).

title: Handling invalid durations — durationy.NA

Use .na() test whether datey NA element, anyNA() test whether element datey NA. durationy version NA NA_durationy_. convenience, constant valid_max_duration maximum valid duration years (2000). performance reasons, intermediate calculations may check NAs.

what: Format or print a datey — format.datey
title: Format or print a datey — format.datey
text: datey printed either YYYY-MM-DD, .e. ISO 8601 extended date format, YYYY-MM-DD.FFF .FFF day fraction part (included even day fraction 0). Note datey created end day (day fraction 1) print start following day.

title: Format or print a datey — format.datey
code: # S3 method for class 'datey' format(x, include_day_fraction = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' print(x, include_day_fraction = TRUE, max = NULL, ...)

title: Format or print a datey — format.datey
text: x datey print format. include_day_fraction Whether include fractional day part. Defaults FALSE. ... arguments passed methods. max Numeric NULL, specifying maximal number entries printed. NULL, getOption("max.print") used. Defaults NULL.

what: Format or print a durationy — format.durationy
title: Format or print a durationy — format.durationy
text: durationy printed decimal. format readable durationy.character().

title: Format or print a durationy — format.durationy
code: # S3 method for class 'durationy' format(x, include_plus = FALSE, use_true_minus = TRUE, year_unit = "yr", ...)  # S3 method for class 'durationy' print(   x,   include_plus = FALSE,   use_true_minus = TRUE,   year_unit = "yr",   max = NULL,   ... )

title: Format or print a durationy — format.durationy
text: x durationy print format. include_plus Whether include plus ('+') sign positive durations. Defaults FALSE. use_true_minus Whether use true minus sign ('−', U+2212) sign opposed ASCII hyphen (-, U+2212). Defaults FALSE. year_unit year unit name print. blank value followed space unit. 20 characters (UTF-8 bytes) contain control characters. Defaults "yr". ... arguments passed methods. max Numeric NULL, specifying maximal number entries printed. NULL, getOption("max.print") used. Defaults NULL.

title: Format or print a durationy — format.durationy
code: pos <- durationy(1)   neg <- durationy(-2.3)   format(pos) # "1 yr" #> [1] "1 yr"   format(pos, include_plus = TRUE) # "1 yr" #> [1] "+1 yr"   format(pos, year_unit = "") # "1" #> [1] "1"   format(neg) # "−2.3 yr" #> [1] "−2.3 yr"   format(neg, use_true_minus = TRUE) # "−2.3 yr" #> [1] "−2.3 yr"   format(neg, year_unit = "a") # "-2.3 a" #> [1] "−2.3 a"

what: Is x a leap year? — is_leap_year
title: Is x a leap year? — is_leap_year
text: Tests whether date year leap year. years outside [1000,3000), returns NA. S3 generic. package provides methods : numeric types double integer (interpreted years), date types datey, Date, POSIXct POSIXlt.

title: Create a datey — point_in_day
text: Create datey year, month, day , point_in_day(), day-fraction. general, prefer explicit start_day(), mid_day() end_day() versions. deconstruct datey, use as_ymdf(). lengths vector arguments must multiples .

title: Create a datey — point_in_day
code: point_in_day(year, month, day, day_fraction, strict = TRUE)  start_day(year, month, day, strict = TRUE)  mid_day(year, month, day, strict = TRUE)  end_day(year, month, day, strict = TRUE)

title: Create a datey — point_in_day
text: year Calendar year. Valid years 1000 (inclusive) 3000 (exclusive). provided double must integers. month Month number calendar year, 1 representing January. provided double must integers. day Day number month, 1 representing first day month. provided double must integers. day_fraction fraction day, [0,1]. 0 means start day, 0.5 means middle day, 1 means end day (identical start next day). strict handle calendar years less 1000 greater equal 3000 day fractions interval [0,1]. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NA arguments result NA regardless strict.)

title: Create a datey — point_in_day
text: following special cases: (year = 999, month = 12, day = 31, day_fraction = 1). cause error, even though theory represents legal datey 1000-01-01.0. (year = 2999, month = 12, day = 31, day_fraction = 1). cause error, even though theory represents illegal datey 3000-01-01.0. (resulting datey invalid though.)
